title: Keel Lorenz and Konrad Lorenz
wikitext:
[]: Keel Lorenz & Konrad Lorenz             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:01 pm I was reading a book about psychology in the library yesterday, and I came across an entry on psychologist Konrad Lorenz , a child psychologist. He spoke in depth about an idea called "imprinting", in which a child learns by bonding with a parent. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Konrad_Lorenz http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imprinting_%28psychology%29 (for more information, as it's too complex to briefly summarize). Anyway, I had a startling idea--what if Hideaki Anno named Kiel (or Keel, whichever you prefer) Lorenz after this man? (K. LORENZ--get it?) The reference to imprinting would make obvious sense, because the lack of parental bonding is almost the most important theme within Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:04 pm I believe you're very right, nice find

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:09 pm this isn't a new theory Reichu or someone like her will have more info, but I don't think Anno-sensei ever acknowledged such a connection

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:29 pm actually I think Anno even openly stated that Keel was named after that guy

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:32 pm The imprinting thing has come up loads of times in connection with the "impression" line in ep.#14. Nobody accepts that the line refers to anything in psychology tho', seeing as it threatens their Rei I in EVA-00 theory or some shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:00 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: this isn't a new theory

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:03 pm Zuggy wrote: Nobody accepts that the line refers to anything in psychology tho'

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:22 pm Yes, Keel was named after this guy. He was even called "Konrad Lorenz" in the Proposal. In before Nazis.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:23 pm if the tank pictures are canon (which I believe that we believe that they are), then Evangelions were created directly from the Seeds.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:26 pm lol wrong thread?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Jun 26, 2008 5:49 pm I don't think my nan would want to part with her radio, HAL. When she dies I can send it to you though, as long as you pay for the postage.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Jun 26, 2008 6:00 pm SAUCE on Anno regarding K. Lorenz now plz

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 26, 2008 6:05 pm You folks need to check out the site moar. (The linked page needs some work, but it serves the purpose.)

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Thu Jun 26, 2008 9:26 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: if the tank pictures are canon (which I believe that we believe that they are), then Evangelions were created directly from the Seeds.

He spoke in depth about an idea called "imprinting", in which a child learns by bonding with a parent. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Konrad_Lorenz http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imprinting

[]: Keel Lorenz & Konrad Lorenz             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Jun 26, 2008 4:01 pm I was reading a book about psychology in the library yesterday, and I came across an entry on psychologist Konrad Lorenz , a child psychologist. He spoke in depth about an idea called "imprinting", in which a child learns by bonding with a parent. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Konrad_Lorenz http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imprinting_%28psychology%29 (for more information, as it's too complex to briefly summarize). Anyway, I had a startling idea--what if Hideaki Anno named Kiel (or Keel, whichever you prefer) Lorenz after this man? (K. LORENZ--get it?) The reference to imprinting would make obvious sense, because the lack of parental bonding is almost the most important theme within Evangelion .
